client_id.description: OpenPanel root-typed Client ID. Sent as the `openpanel-client-id` header. Falls back to `OPENPANEL_CLIENT_ID`. Used when `oidc` and `token` are not configured.

client_secret.description: OpenPanel root-typed Client Secret (the `sec_*` value). Sent as the `openpanel-client-secret` header. Falls back to `OPENPANEL_CLIENT_SECRET`.

host.description: Base URL of the OpenPanel API (e.g. `https://analytics.example.com`). The provider appends `/api/manage/...` for requests. Falls back to the `OPENPANEL_HOST` environment variable.

token.description: Static OIDC Bearer token. The provider passes this through as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` without refreshing. Falls back to `OPENPANEL_TOKEN`. Use this when an external tool already manages JWT issuance.

audience.description: Requested audience for the issued JWT. Must match the OpenPanel api pod's `ADMIN_OIDC_AUDIENCE`. Falls back to `OPENPANEL_OIDC_AUDIENCE`.

client_id.description: OIDC ServiceUser client ID. Falls back to `OPENPANEL_OIDC_CLIENT_ID`.

client_secret.description: OIDC ServiceUser client secret. Falls back to `OPENPANEL_OIDC_CLIENT_SECRET`.

issuer.description: OIDC issuer base URL (e.g. `https://auth.example.com`). The provider fetches `<issuer>/.well-known/openid-configuration` to discover the token endpoint. Falls back to `OPENPANEL_OIDC_ISSUER`.

block.description: Authenticate via the OAuth2 `client_credentials` grant against an OIDC issuer. The provider mints a JWT, caches it until ~60s before expiry, then refreshes transparently. Requires the OpenPanel api pod to have `ADMIN_OIDC_ISSUER` configured.
Terraform provider for OpenPanel (https://openpanel.dev). Manages Projects, Clients, and References via the `/manage` REST API.

cors.description: List of allowed origins (write clients only).

id.description: Client ID assigned by OpenPanel. Used as the SDK's `clientId` and the `openpanel-client-id` header value.

name.description: Human-readable name shown in the OpenPanel dashboard.

secret.description: The `sec_*` secret. Populated only at create-time; subsequent reads return null.

type.description: One of `read`, `write`, `root`. Drives which API surfaces accept this Client. Immutable after create.

block.description: An OpenPanel SDK credential (Client). `type=write` is what your front-end SDK ships with; `type=read` reads analytics out via the API; `type=root` can call `/manage`. The secret is returned only on create.

id.description: Organization ID. Slugified from the name when not specified at create-time.

name.description: Human-readable Organization name. Shown in the OpenPanel dashboard.

timezone.description: IANA timezone (e.g. `America/Chicago`). Drives the dashboard's default reporting window.

block.description: An OpenPanel Organization. The top-level tenant primitive; scopes Projects, Clients, References, and Members.

cors.description: List of allowed origins for the Project's write clients (the JS SDK CORS allowlist).

domain.description: Primary site domain associated with the Project. Optional metadata.

name.description: Human-readable Project name. Shown in the OpenPanel dashboard.

organization_id.description: ID of the Organization this Project belongs to. Inferred from the provider's root Client credential.

block.description: An OpenPanel Project. Projects scope SDK clients and event references; they are the unit a tenant app integrates against.
A Reference annotates an event in the OpenPanel timeline (deploy, campaign, incident). Surfaces on dashboards alongside the analytics.
